subHeadline: The comparisons Mercedes-Benz buyers actually want — generation vs generation, engine vs engine, model vs model. Honest, data-backed, and built for real-world decision-making.

comparisons.title: W204 vs W205 vs W206 C-Class
comparisons.preview: Which C-Class generation to buy — the verdict on reliability, running costs, and long-term value. The W205 post-facelift is the sweet spot; the W204 is the bargain-with-risk; the W206 is the safest.

comparisons.title: OM651 vs OM654 Diesel
comparisons.preview: The rear-mounted "Black Death" chain risk vs the revised modern design. OM651: 1,250 enquiries in 2025, mostly injector seal and chain-related [MB-VERIFIED]. OM654: 520 enquiries, materially fewer failure reports [MB-VERIFIED]. The OM654 wins on reliability; the OM651 wins on purchase price with documented history.

comparisons.title: OM642 vs M276 Petrol/Diesel
comparisons.preview: The V6 diesel workhorse vs the modern V6 petrol. OM642: 880 enquiries, labour-intensive but well-understood oil cooler seal issue [MB-VERIFIED]. M276: 390 enquiries, timing chain stretch on earlier examples [MB-VERIFIED]. Choose on running costs, not reliability — both are sound if maintained.

comparisons.title: C-Class vs E-Class
comparisons.preview: Which offers better value for money over 3–5 years? They share engines, so buy on condition and generation, not badge. The E-Class is roomier and more refined; the C-Class is cheaper to buy and run.

comparisons.title: GLC vs GLE
comparisons.preview: The mid-size SUV vs the large SUV — which one should you own? GLC X253: 304 enquiries [MB-VERIFIED]. GLE W166: 142 enquiries [MB-VERIFIED]. The GLC is cheaper to run; the GLE offers more space and towing capacity.

comparisons.title: Diesel vs Petrol Running Costs
comparisons.preview: Which fuel type is cheaper to own in 2026? Diesel: better motorway economy but the OM651's known injector-seal and chain risk. Petrol (M276/M264): lower purchase price sensitivity but higher fuel costs. The OM654 diesel is the modern motorway champion.

bottomCta.text: Not sure which is right for you? Use our diagnostic tool to get a personalised verdict in minutes.
